name: Swedish Lapphund
description: The Swedish Lapphund is the oldest of the Scandinavian Lapphund breeds and Sweden's oldest native breed, descended directly from dogs that accompanied the Sami people across northern Sweden for thousands of years. Unlike the Finnish and Norwegian Lapphunds which also appear in other colors, the Swedish Lapphund appears almost exclusively in solid black or liver-brown. This versatile herding and hunting dog was used by the Sami to hunt bear and elk as well as to herd reindeer, but is today primarily a companion and show dog rarely seen outside Scandinavia.

conditions_detail.name: Progressive Retinal Atrophy (PRA)
conditions_detail.description: PRA is a group of hereditary diseases causing progressive degeneration of the photoreceptor cells in the retina. It leads to night blindness first, followed by complete blindness. There is no treatment, but DNA tests exist for many forms.
conditions_detail.treatment_overview: There is no effective treatment or cure for PRA. Management focuses on adapting the dog's environment to maintain quality of life as vision deteriorates.
prevention_tips: DNA test breeding stock before mating
Only breed clear or carrier-to-clear pairings

conditions_detail.name: Hip Dysplasia
conditions_detail.description: Hip dysplasia is a genetic condition where the hip joint doesn't develop properly, causing the ball and socket to not fit together correctly. This leads to joint deterioration, pain, and arthritis over time.
conditions_detail.treatment_overview: Treatment ranges from weight management and physical therapy for mild cases to total hip replacement for severe cases. NSAIDs and joint supplements help manage pain.
prevention_tips: Screen breeding stock with hip scoring

conditions_detail.name: Diabetes Mellitus
conditions_detail.description: Canine diabetes mellitus is a metabolic disease caused by insufficient insulin production (type 1-like) leading to persistent hyperglycaemia. Unlike cats, dogs rarely achieve remission and require lifelong insulin therapy.
conditions_detail.treatment_overview: Twice-daily insulin injections aligned with meals form the cornerstone of treatment. Consistent diet and exercise routines are essential. Regular glucose curve monitoring guides dose adjustments.
prevention_tips: Spay females as progesterone worsens insulin resistance
